narrative: Eight PM on a Sunday in Billings, the last light gone behind the Rimrocks. Jolene finishes the novel, sets it down, and does nothing for fifteen minutes. Then she picks up her phone and texts Amber: ‘You were right about the ending. I didn’t see it coming but I should have.’ It’s the first text between them. Amber reads it in bed with her glasses on and smiles and types back something Jolene will read three times. The city is settling. Ryan’s kids are at their mom’s house and he’s alone in the quiet of it, grading film. Cole’s dog is asleep under the kitchen table, legs twitching. Eli is packing a bag, trying to figure out what he owns that actually matters. Nora is in bed with the light on, not reading, just lying there. Marcus is home from his shift, showered, sitting on the couch in the dark. The incident report has been sent. Whatever comes next, comes next. Out past the city limits, on the Rimrocks, something moves in the dark with the patience of a thing that has been here longer than any of them.
events: Jolene finishes the novel Amber recommended and sits with it for a long time before texting Amber a single line about the ending

The weekend closes out: characters settle in for the night, the mountain lion is still out there, the stray dog is asleep under Cole’s table, and Marcus’s incident report is sitting in someone’s inbox
